title: Visualizing Graph Types, Incidence, and Vertex Degree
description: A diagram contrasting finite, infinite, and bipartite graphs while illustrating incidence, degree, and an isolated vertex.
elements: Bipartite Graph (Two distinct vertex sets U and V)
relationships: The Finite Graph shows vertices with defined degree (e.g., deg=2)
The Infinite Graph demonstrates a structure that continues indefinitely
The Bipartite Graph shows all edges only connect vertices from set U to set V
The Isolated Vertex has no incident edges, showing deg(v)=0
Edges demonstrate incidence by connecting specific pairs of vertices
